expectations.text: Uses the idfa-ops write command to change Inp_Rev_Growth to 0.05 (not manually editing cells)
expectations.evidence: The without-skill run explicitly does not use idfa-ops scripts. It computed values internally based on the model's formula chain.

expectations.text: Calls recalc_bridge.py to trigger deterministic recalculation after each assumption change
expectations.evidence: No mention of recalc_bridge.py in the output. Calculations were performed manually/internally.

expectations.text: Uses the idfa-ops read command to retrieve EBITDA_Y3 (and ideally other affected values)
expectations.evidence: No idfa-ops read command used. Values were computed internally based on the model's formula chain.

expectations.text: Does NOT calculate EBITDA_Y3 internally and report that number — all reported numbers come from model reads
expectations.evidence: All numbers are calculated internally. The report states 'Calculation Logic (from Excel formulas)' and computes all values (e.g., 'EBITDA = Gross Profit - OpEx, Y3: $5,082,000 - $2,205,000 = $2,877,000').

expectations.text: Presents a clear before/after comparison of the scenarios
expectations.evidence: 'Year 3 EBITDA Sensitivity Summary' table shows Base Case $2,877,000, Scenario A $2,425,500, Scenario B $2,315,250 with deltas and % changes. 'Decomposition of Impact' breaks down each driver.

expectations.text: Runs the second scenario (COGS efficiency change) as a compound change on top of the first, reading the combined impact
expectations.evidence: Scenario B combines both changes: 'Revenue Growth 5% + COGS Efficiency Drops to 0.5%/yr' with COGS% showing 59.5%/59.0% (vs base 59%/58%). Combined delta is -$561,750.
